question.question: Based on the detonation report, which of the following process injection sub-techniques are directly evidenced by specific API calls, memory operations, or monitored target observations in this sample?
options: A. Process Injection via writing data to a remote process (T1055) — evidenced by hash.exe writing 1500 bytes to a remote process
B. Thread Execution Hijacking (T1055.003) — evidenced by rundll32.exe creating a thread in process -1
C. Dynamic-link Library Injection (T1055.001) — evidenced by FreeLibrary API string reference and MITRE mapping
D. Process Injection via virtual memory allocation in a remote process (T1055) — evidenced by hash.exe allocating memory in C:\hash.exe
E. Process Hollowing (T1055.012) — evidenced by CreateProcessA followed by memory unmapping in the spawned process
F. Extra Window Memory Injection (T1055.011) — evidenced by FindWindowA usage mapped to T1055.015/T1055.011 in MITRE ATT&CK
G. APC Injection (T1055.004) — evidenced by QueueUserAPC API calls observed in the process tree
H. ListPlanting (T1055.015) — evidenced by FindWindowA searching for class #32770 and MITRE ATT&CK mapping T1055.015
